input.background: Trees were surveyed for the presence of lichen. Each lichen found was recorded as either occurring on the north, east, south or west quadrant of the tree: 36 lichens were found on north-facing quadrants, 50 on east-facing quadrants, 29 on south-facing quadrants and 45 on west-facing quadrants.
input.question: What can you conclude about the distribution of lichens on trees?

output.answer: There is no significant departure from random orientation in lichens.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
